description:
The typer's spec-discovery fixed point only sees what the LUB
view (`by_fn_idx[F]`) produces. It never sees what the per-spec view
(`specs[(F, key)]`) would produce. Narrow specs that are TIGHTER
than the LUB never get registered — every recursive callsite
resolves to the wider spec, dragging Tagged ABI through the chain.

Discovered via fz-ul4.32.1's annotated CLIF dump: `fact_5.fz`'s
`fact` registers `[5]` and `[int | float]` but NOT `[int]`, even
though `fact_s2`'s body computes `n - 1` as `int` (we verified
inline at the spec's terminator). The fixpoint just doesn't read
from per-spec views when collecting `callsite_keys`.

## ELI5

The typer has two views of every fn F:
  1. `by_fn_idx[F]` — F typed under the LUB of every arg type ever
     seen at any call site (union of all callers).
  2. `specs[(F, key)]` — F typed under specifically `key`. One per
     registered narrow spec.

The fixed-point loop walks `by_fn_idx` to discover new
`callsite_keys`. For each fn body it asks "what arg-tuples does
this body pass to its callees?" — but only under the LUB view.

For fact:
  - Iter 1: `by_fn_idx[fact]` is typed under `n: any` (initial).
    Body has `fact(n - 1)` → `numeric_result(any, 1) = int | float`.
    callsite_keys[fact] += `[int | float]`. main: `[5]`.
    Register `specs[(fact, [5])]` + `specs[(fact, [int | float])]`.
  - Iter 2: `by_fn_idx[fact]` retyped under
    `n: 5 ∪ int|float = int | float`. Body still emits
    `int | float`. No new keys. Done.

The view `specs[(fact, [5])]` exists and computes `int` for the
recursive arg. But the fixpoint never reads from `specs` when
collecting `callsite_keys` for direct Call / TailCall. So `[int]`
is never registered, and every recursive site resolves to
`[int | float]`.

`.29.12.2` already walks per-spec bodies to discover
`MakeClosure` capture keys (line 285). The fix is the same shape
for direct Call / TailCall: in the per-spec walk, also record the
arg-Descr tuple at every Term::Call / Term::TailCall under the
spec's per-block env.

## Hypothesis-check

For fact_5.fz, after the fix:
  - `specs[(fact, [5])]` body has `fact(n - 1)` with `n: 5` →
    arg Descr = `int`. callsite_keys[fact] += `[int]`.
  - Register `specs[(fact, [int])]`. Body re-typed with `n: int`.
  - That body's `fact(n - 1)` with `n: int` → `numeric_result(int,
    1) = int`. callsite_keys[fact] += `[int]` (already there).
  - The `[int | float]` spec stays registered (any-key pass still
    produces it on iter 1) but nobody resolves to it any more —
    `[int]` strictly subsumes it for queries from `[5]` and `[int]`.

## Fix location

src/ir_typer.rs, in the existing per-spec walk near line 285
(currently `for (caller_fn_id, caller_key) in &spec_snapshot`).
Inside the per-block loop, after the stmt-env replay, inspect
b.terminator for Term::Call / Term::TailCall and record:
  callsite_keys.entry(*callee).or_default().insert(key)
where `key` is the per-spec env's view of each arg.

This must run BEFORE the `if !specs.contains_key(...)` registration
block (line 366) so newly-discovered keys land in the same iter.

## Acceptance

1. fact_5.fz dumps show:
     ; fn fact_s_int
     ;   @spec   fact(int) -> 1
     ;   @key    [int]
     ;   @abi    (RawInt) -> RawInt
   and fact_s2 / fact_s_int's recursive call resolves to the
   [int] spec (no Tagged in the recursive chain).

2. add1.fz golden: ishl_imm/bor_imm at the cont seam still TBD
   (that's the cont/halt seam, fz-ul4.27.22.x). The producer's
   `n - 1` site won't be tagging into the recursive call any more.

3. Full test suite passes; no spec-count regression in fixture
   tests that count specs.

## Risk

Spec count grows. Programs with deep recursion + literal entries
could see many narrow specs (one per literal). Mitigation: the
existing `widen` step at iter ≥ widen_at already widens singletons
to ranges; the new per-spec walk inherits this naturally. Watch
fixture compile times — if a fixture explodes, we know where.

## Out of scope

- Cont seam typing (fz-ul4.27.22.2). The narrow seams it wants
  will now actually exist once this lands.
- Typed halt-cont (fz-ul4.27.22.3). Same.
- Pruning the dead `[int | float]` spec. Optional; subsumption
  resolver makes it harmless.
title: fz-typer.spec-pollution: register narrow specs via per-spec walk
